frameworks.name: neural alignment / inter-subject representational similarity
frameworks.one_line: Shared canonical neural patterns across learners and experts index successful learning

frameworks.name: expert-novice representational comparison
frameworks.one_line: Alignment to experts extends student-student ISC as a learning marker

core_question: Does the degree to which a learner's neural representations align with experts and peers predict learning outcomes in a real-world course?
key_assumptions: successful learning requires forming the right neural representations
shared canonical activity patterns across individuals reflect correct conceptual structure
manipulations: longitudinal scanning across an introductory CS course
comparison group: graduate CS experts vs. novice students
key_findings: between-student neural alignment predicts final exam performance
within-student, concepts evoking higher alignment with experts yield better learning
concept-specific alignment with peers also tracks learning outcomes
neural patterns associated with specific learned concepts are identifiable in individuals
interpretations: learning is mirrored in convergence toward shared expert-like neural representations
neural alignment can serve as a real-life, online assay of conceptual learning
